reason_of_clustering: These entities represent future contracts for live cattle that are traded on different expiration months. They are grouped together because they pertain to the same underlying asset (live cattle) and investors often analyze them collectively to understand price trends and market conditions in the cattle sector.

Similar to live cattle futures, these entities represent future contracts for feeder cattle, also traded on different expiration months. They are grouped due to their direct relevance to the cattle market, allowing investors to assess trends in the feeder cattle segment specifically and make comparisons with live cattle futures for a more informed trading strategy.

These contracts are collateralized on a month-to-month basis, reflecting seasonal trends and market behaviors in livestock trading. This cluster helps investors analyze the near-term versus medium-term expectations in cattle pricing and trading activity.
